purpose: Score Galaxy Guardians against local playtest perception of Galaxian-like sound, pace, and visual identity, separate from evidence coverage. This artifact intentionally lowers the apparent preview score where the current build is measurable but not yet compelling.

sourceEvidence.playtestContext: Local browser review of the development-only Galaxy Guardians preview on the MacBook workstation.
playtestFindings: Sound is nothing like the original Galaxians.
Pace feels off and slow in various ways.
Graphics still feel pretty far from the original.

metricScale.0: No recognizable Galaxian relationship in play.
metricScale.5: Recognizable reference intent exists, but the player still reads it as a rough approximation.
metricScale.7: Compelling preview: a player immediately understands the Galaxian family resemblance even before final extraction.
metricScale.10: Reference-faithful enough for public conformance claims.

summary.releaseRead: Closer, but still not yet compelling enough for beta-facing preview. The runtime now has measured stage-rank pressure, component-derived sprites, and labeled cue-window influenced audio. Audio and motion feel still need live browser/playback review before a beta-facing claim.

categories.label: Audio character and Galaxian acoustic feel
metricSet: event mix density during live play
categories.currentRead: Cue ownership and shape are harnessed, CPU analysis finds 64 mixed-source cue candidates, 8 cue windows are now promoted with waveform/spectrogram previews, and the runtime cues were retuned toward the measured square/noise bands. This is much closer architecturally, but still needs human listening and live playback review.
categories.nextEvidenceNeeded: Human-listen the promoted cue windows, replace dirty mixed-gameplay windows, and score live playback against the target set.

categories.label: Motion pace and lower-field pressure
categories.currentRead: The latest pass shortened formation entry, first dive, flagship pressure, scout intervals, shot cadence, and bottom wrap timing, then added measured stage-rank pressure so stages three and five carry earlier dives, faster dives, faster shots, and more event density while stage one remains anchored.
categories.nextEvidenceNeeded: Use browser-reviewed runtime traces beside reference tracklets, then promote sprite-recognized dive paths and rack timing into target bands.

categories.label: Graphic alignment and alien/player likeness
categories.currentRead: Sprites are now tuned from component crop targets for flagship, escort, scout, and player ship while remaining pack-owned and separate from Aurora. They should read much more like the Galaxian family, but still need visual browser review at actual gameplay scale.
categories.nextEvidenceNeeded: Human-review the component crop PNGs and runtime playfield scale, then adjust any sprite that feels too dense or too literal.

categories.label: Platform boundary and preview safety
metricSet: Platinum APIs only for shared shell behavior
categories.currentRead: The preview remains cleanly application-owned and dev-only while using Platinum as the shell/platform layer.
categories.nextEvidenceNeeded: Keep boundary gates mandatory as the gameplay slice becomes more complete.

recommendedNext: Human-listen the promoted Galaxian shot, dive, enemy-shot, life-loss, and hit cue windows and replace dirty labels.
Browser-review the component-derived sprites at real gameplay scale and adjust density/readability where needed.
Run a browser-reviewed pacing pass after the current faster runtime is built, with perceived urgency scored beside runtime trace metrics.
Keep Guardians beta-facing preview blocked until playtest-weighted score reaches at least 7.0/10.
